ead.archdesc:
======= RMM08067International Graffiti Times archive8067Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
January 20172B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:06:52 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
International Graffiti Times archive
Schmidlapp, David.
Phase2.
8067
15 cubic feet. 15 cubic feet.
1970-2000. Printed material, manuscripts, photographs, publications. Collection includes 44 original, hand-collaged past-up boards from which the IGTimes page layouts were reproduced; hundreds of original manuscript, typescript, and desktop printing documents comprising the text files from which its editorial content was drawn; more than 4000 original photographs of aerosol street art submitted to the magazine by a community of international aerosol writers and readers; ca. 5000 original reader submissions; ca. 500 original magazines and fanzines; 150 clippings from newspapers and magazines; the archive of the Aerosol Art Armada, which includes ca. 1,000 35 mm. slide transparencies; a selection of flyers for parties and openings in New York City; 5 file folders with information from scholarly researchers who contacted the IGTimes for information on street art. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 24 ? (unprocessed) ======= 24 ? (unprocessed) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 7623873
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
January 2017
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, January 2017
Date modified:
Fredrika Loew, October 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
In late 1983, downtown artist and photographer David Schmidlapp published the first issue of the IGTimes under the name "Yanqui Junkie." The International Graffiti Times was "the original inner-city, international, aerosol 'zine,' and the Aerosol Art Armada traveling show, providing a view of the development of aerosol street art and the rise of hip hop culture in New York City and around the world from the 1970s to the 1990s.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Collection includes 44 original, hand-collaged past-up boards from which the IGTimes page layouts were reproduced; hundreds of original manuscript, typescript, and desktop printing documents comprising the text files from which its editorial content was drawn; more than 4000 original photographs of aerosol street art submitted to the magazine by a community of international aerosol writers and readers; ca. 5000 original reader submissions; ca. 500 original magazines and fanzines; 150 clippings from newspapers and magazines; the archive of the Aerosol Art Armada, which includes ca. 1,000 35 mm. slide transparencies; a selection of flyers for parties and openings in New York City; 5 file folders with information from scholarly researchers who contacted the IGTimes for information on street art.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Magazines and Fanzines
Series II. Aerosol Art Armada
Series III. Photographs
Series IV. Magazine and Newspaper Clippings
Series V. Editorial Copy Files
Series VI. Flyers
Series VI. Flyers
Series VII. Correspondence
Cite As:
International Graffiti Times Archive, #8067. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Street art. Graffiti. Artists. Hip-hop. Street photography. African Americans. African American art Phase2.

Scope and Contents
Includes Rock and Roll, Bronx Museum, NY Press, John Nation, and New Museum.Aerosol Armada paste-upsUndated139Series III. Photographs
Scope and Contents
Contains original photographs of aerosol street art, many submitted to the magazine by a community of international 'aerosol' writers and other readers, others shot by Schmidlapp and the magazine's inner circle and a few from professional photographers documenting the various scenes.
Many folders also contain flyers and correspondence.Early Europe/England, pre-aerosol1982-1985147Miscellaneous EuropeUndated148-12Visit to West Hollywood and East LA, plus Venice Beach19861413Schmidlapp photo plusUndated1414Koch photos, IG TimesUndated1415NYC Trains and WallsUndated1416-17England- UKUndated1418-19AustraliaUndated1420-21Miscellaneous EuropeUndated151Volume 10Undated152Volume 11Undated153Volume 12-13Undated154Volume 14Undated155Volume 15Undated156Phase and Vulcan plusUndated157-8CaliUndated159-12America: Philly, Chicago, Miami, etc.Undated1513-15Reproduced TrainsUndated1516Early Cali1983-1987161Hardcore- Morgan WalkerUndated162Canvases- Events- Black and WhiteUndated163Black and white trainsUndated164-5Michael Lawrence, Trains, USA, black and white1970-1979166Black and whiteUndated167Letters with photosUndated168-10UnlabelledUndated1611-13UnlabelledUndated171Letters with photos, many written by Cris MendozaUndated173-6Series IV. Magazine and Newspaper ClippingsClips: Outside NYC1985-19901311Xeroxes: Newsclips NYC1988-19941312Copies of clippings1973-1994177Newspapers and clippings1984-1998194
Scope and Contents

Scope and Contents
Tox, Theftway, Vibe, Vilalge Beat, Voice, Yacata, WaxSeries V. Editorial Copy Files
Scope and Contents
Consists of manuscripts, typescript and desktop printing documents comprising the core of the magazine's editorial content for each of the 15 issues, including interview transcripts and notes for articles.Current IGT letters, proposals, statements1980-19901614Volume 1: copy19831615Volume 219841616Volume 319841617Volume 419841618Volume 51984-19851619Volume 619851620Volume 71986-19871621Volume 81986-19871622Volume 919871623Volume 101988-19891624Volume 111986-19901625Volume 12 and 131990-19911626Volume 14Undated1627Volume 15Undated1628UnpublishedUndated1629IGT xeroxes of issues/posters1990-1995181Xeroxes of press clips and covers of press1985-1995182IGT Promo writings: Schmidlapp1985-1990183Flyers, letters, IGT xeroxUndated186Rockathon entries19871313Rockathon entries, Phase 219871314StickersUndated1823Series VI. FlyersFlyers and pamphlets1980-19992014-16
Scope and Contents
Includes some business cardsA selection of fliers for parties, openings, and events in New York City featuring the magazine itself and members of the IGTimes community, including correspondence with alternative art spaces ABC no Rio, Fashion Moda Gallery, Franklin Furnace and Food Stamp Gallery, ca. 1984-1994.Undated172Flyers, art, lettersUndated1310Flyers, clippings, and sections from zinesUndated2012-13
Scope and Contents
Includes Writer's Resource Guide, War Craft, New Urbanism, Café Armageddon, 14k, Bullshit MonthlyArtworkUndated191Flyers, "Get Hipped"Undated192Flyers and artworkUndated193Series VII. Correspondence
Scope and Contents
Consists mainly of reader submissions including letters, postcards, original artwork, photographs and other items from local and international correspondents, as well as mail art, prisoner and G.I. letters, and subscription requestsLetters1980-1987187Letters1985-1989188Letters from prisons and some military1987-1995189Letters from prisons and some military19961810Letters from prisons and some military19971811-13Letters from prisons and some military1998-20081814Letters from prisons and some military1987-20081815Letters1987-19901816Letters1990-19991817-18Writings, letters, interviews, by VulcanUndated178Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGTUndated179Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1989-19901710Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT19911711Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT19921712-15Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1993201-3Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1994204Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1995205Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1996206-8Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1997209-13Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1997211Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1998212-4Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1999215Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT2000-2001216Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT2002-2005217Letters from Mark Moore1989-1997218Empty envelopes1989-2005219Letters from John Grimes1995-19982110IGT correspondence used in "Now Scream" exhibition1989-19942111Mail art1987-19881819-20

Scope and Contents
Includes Rock and Roll, Bronx Museum, NY Press, John Nation, and New Museum.Aerosol Armada paste-upsUndated139Series III. Photographs
Scope and Contents
Contains original photographs of aerosol street art, many submitted to the magazine by a community of international 'aerosol' writers and other readers, others shot by Schmidlapp and the magazine's inner circle and a few from professional photographers documenting the various scenes.
Many folders also contain flyers and correspondence.Early Europe/England, pre-aerosol1982-1985147Miscellaneous EuropeUndated148-12Visit to West Hollywood and East LA, plus Venice Beach19861413Schmidlapp photo plusUndated1414Koch photos, IG TimesUndated1415NYC Trains and WallsUndated1416-17England- UKUndated1418-19AustraliaUndated1420-21Miscellaneous EuropeUndated151Volume 10Undated152Volume 11Undated153Volume 12-13Undated154Volume 14Undated155Volume 15Undated156Phase and Vulcan plusUndated157-8CaliUndated159-12America: Philly, Chicago, Miami, etc.Undated1513-15Reproduced TrainsUndated1516Early Cali1983-1987161Hardcore- Morgan WalkerUndated162Canvases- Events- Black and WhiteUndated163Black and white trainsUndated164-5Michael Lawrence, Trains, USA, black and white1970-1979166Black and whiteUndated167Letters with photosUndated168-10UnlabelledUndated1611-13UnlabelledUndated171Letters with photos, many written by Cris MendozaUndated173-6Series IV. Scope and Contents
Consists of manuscripts, typescript and desktop printing documents comprising the core of the magazine's editorial content for each of the 15 issues, including interview transcripts and notes for articles.Current IGT letters, proposals, statements1980-19901614Volume 1: copy19831615Volume 219841616Volume 319841617Volume 419841618Volume 51984-19851619Volume 619851620Volume 71986-19871621Volume 81986-19871622Volume 919871623Volume 101988-19891624Volume 111986-19901625Volume 12 and 131990-19911626Volume 14Undated1627Volume 15Undated1628UnpublishedUndated1629IGT xeroxes of issues/posters1990-1995181Xeroxes of press clips and covers of press1985-1995182IGT Promo writings: Schmidlapp1985-1990183Flyers, letters, IGT xeroxUndated186Rockathon entries19871313Rockathon entries, Phase 219871314StickersUndated1823Series VI. FlyersFlyers and pamphlets1980-19992014-16
Scope and Contents
Includes some business cardsA selection of fliers for parties, openings, and events in New York City featuring the magazine itself and members of the IGTimes community, including correspondence with alternative art spaces ABC no Rio, Fashion Moda Gallery, Franklin Furnace and Food Stamp Gallery, ca. 1984-1994.Undated172Flyers, art, lettersUndated1310Flyers, clippings, and sections from zinesUndated2012-13

Scope and Contents
Consists mainly of reader submissions including letters, postcards, original artwork, photographs and other items from local and international correspondents, as well as mail art, prisoner and G.I. letters, and subscription requestsLetters1980-1987187Letters1985-1989188Letters from prisons and some military1987-1995189Letters from prisons and some military19961810Letters from prisons and some military19971811-13Letters from prisons and some military1998-20081814Letters from prisons and some military1987-20081815Letters1987-19901816Letters1990-19991817-18Writings, letters, interviews, by VulcanUndated178Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGTUndated179Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1989-19901710Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT19911711Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT19921712-15Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1993201-3Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1994204Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1995205Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1996206-8Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1997209-13Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1997211Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1998212-4Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT1999215Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT2000-2001216Letters with envelopes, some sent by IGT2002-2005217Letters from Mark Moore1989-1997218Empty envelopes1989-2005219Letters from John Grimes1995-19982110IGT correspondence used in "Now Scream" exhibition1989-19942111Mail art1987-19881819-20
